rationale: The cited paper appears directly relevant to a core construct in the citing paper: both focus on hedonic (vs. non-hedonic/utilitarian) choice processes, and the citing paper’s theory centers on chronic hedonic goals in self-control conflict. Given this strong construct overlap and the cited paper’s role as an established empirical account of hedonic–utilitarian trade-offs, it is plausible as a theory/foundation source rather than an incidental mention.
